_meta.note: Phase 1 placeholder seed. Three sample projects shaped exactly like AGOL feature service responses. Chunk 1.2 replaces this with a live export of all 39 Data Program projects from the tracker.

goal_descriptions.governance: Putting policies, standards, and accountable ownership in place for how the city manages its data.
goal_descriptions.quality: Making the city's data trustworthy, current, and findable for the teams that need it.
goal_descriptions.security: Protecting sensitive data, enforcing retention, and meeting compliance requirements.
goal_descriptions.literacy: Growing the city's confidence with data through training, documentation, and shared practice.
goal_descriptions.architecture: Building the technical foundation the rest of the data program runs on.
goal_descriptions.business-needs: Surfacing what departments actually need from data so investments target real work.

projects.title: MDW to Snowflake migration
projects.leadership_summary: Replaces the legacy MDW data warehouse with Snowflake, modernizing the data infrastructure for every department that consumes city data. Once complete, downstream analytics, reporting, and integration work can move faster and at lower operational cost.
projects.description: Migrate non-geometric watergis and city-wide reporting workloads from MDW to Snowflake.
projects.problem_statement: MDW is reaching end-of-life and limits the city's ability to scale its analytics workloads.
projects.dp_goal: Implementing Scalable Architecture and Technology, Enhance Data Quality and Accessibility

projects.title: Application Catalog for the Data Team
projects.leadership_summary: A single, searchable catalog of every data product the team produces — dashboards, applications, datasets — so departments can find what already exists before requesting new work.
projects.description: Build the catalog with metadata, ownership, and usage tracking.
projects.problem_statement: Teams don't know what data products already exist, leading to duplicate requests.

projects.title: GIS and Spatial Analysis Training Course Series
projects.leadership_title: GIS and spatial analysis training program
projects.leadership_summary: Final cohort certified. 47 staff across 12 departments now equipped to use the city's GIS tools without ad-hoc support requests.
projects.description: Course series for analyst staff covering ArcGIS Pro, online platforms, and spatial analysis fundamentals.
projects.problem_statement: City staff lacked structured GIS training, creating dependency on the data team for routine mapping tasks.
